title: 10 best herbal teas for cystitis

remedy_properties: Uricosuric activity: Bearberry tea has been shown to increase the excretion of urates from the body, which can help reduce the concentration of uric acid in the urine and alleviate symptoms of cystitis.

Antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial compounds present in bearberry tea, such as arbutin, have been found to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause urinary tract infections (UTIs), including Escherichia coli (E. coli) and Staphylococcus saprophyticus.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Bearberry tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the bladder and urinary tract associated with cystitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arctostaphylos uva ursi, also known as bearberry, teas helps with cystitis because it contains arbutin, a natural compound that has antibacterial properties. These properties help to combat the bacterial infections that often cause cystitis. Additionally, bearberry teas have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis. By promoting a healthy urinary tract and reducing bacterial growth, bearberry teas can provide relief and support for those suffering from cystitis.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that helps reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections in the urinary tract.
Arbutin: A phenolic glycoside with antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that helps reduce oxidative stress, combat bacterial infections, and alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
Tannins: A type of polyphenol with astringent and antimicrobial properties that helps reduce inflammation, combat bacterial infections, and alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make teas for cystitis due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks are used to make teas for cystitis as they contain compounds that help soothe and protect the urinary tract.
Fruits: The fruits are used to make teas for cystitis, likely due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves and berries from a reliable source in late summer.
Rinse the harvested plant material with cold water to remove dirt and debris quickly.
Combine the 1/4 cup of plant material with 1 quart of boiling water in a heat-resistant container immediately.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid has reached a comfortable temperature patiently.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids completely.

remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root contains a thickening agent called mucilage, which helps to soothe and protect the urinary tract lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The herb contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of cystitis such as burning sensation while urinating.

Astringent properties: Marshmallow tea's astringent properties can help constrict the urinary tract and reduce the frequency and urgency of urination, providing relief from the discomfort associated with cystitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, teas helps with cystitis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The mucilages present in the herb create a protective barrier on the bladder and urinary tract walls, reducing inflammation and discomfort. This natural barrier also helps to prevent further irritation and infection. As a result, Althaea officinalis tea may provide relief from the burning sensation, frequent urination, and other symptoms associated with cystitis, promoting a more comfortable and pain-free experience.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes of the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis.
Althaea polysaccharides: Althaea polysaccharides have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and irritation in the bladder and urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of cystitis.

Glycosides (specifically, Althaea officinalis flavonoids): Glycosides, particularly flavonoids, exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which help to combat infections and reduce oxidative stress that can contribute to cystitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and protect the urinary tract.
Leaves: The leaves contain similar mucilaginous properties to the roots, making them a suitable alternative for teas and infusions.
Barks: The barks of Althaea officinalis are also used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-4 grams of dried althaea officinalis root and 1 cup of boiling water for steeping.
Combine the dried root in a heat-resistant cup or teapot with the boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the flavors and medicinal properties to infuse.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup.
Serve the tea hot or chilled within 15-30 minutes of preparation and consumption.

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Antioxidants in corn tea, such as polyphenols, help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, which can contribute to the development of cystitis.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids found in corn tea have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis, such as burning sensation while urinating, frequency, and urgency.

Antimicrobial activity: The antimicrobial properties of corn tea, particularly the presence of beta-carotene and other carotenoids, may help combat bacterial infections in the urinary tract that can contribute to cystitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zea mays, also known as corn, teas helps with cystitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The flavonoids present in Zea mays have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria, which can contribute to cystitis. Additionally, the tea's high water content helps to flush out bacteria and toxins from the urinary tract, promoting healing and reducing symptoms of cystitis. remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They may help alleviate cystitis symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract.

remedy_parts: Stems: The stems of Zea mays are used to make teas for cystitis due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Zea mays are used to make teas for cystitis because of their diuretic and soothing effects on the urinary tract.
Roots: The roots of Zea mays are used to make teas for cystitis due to their ability to help reduce inflammation and promote urine production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh zea mays leaves in the early morning or late afternoon.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly with lukewarm water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steam the leaves in a pot for 5-7 minutes to release their medicinal properties.
Combine the steamed leaves with 1 quart of boiling water and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a cup to remove the solids.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Goldenrod tea has been shown to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis such as burning and frequency.
Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antifungal compounds present in goldenrod tea can help combat infections that cause cystitis, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Uric acid-reducing properties: Goldenrod has been traditionally used to reduce uric acid levels in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis caused by excess uric acid crystals in the urinary tract.

The active compounds in Solidago virgaurea, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections. Additionally, the tea's diuretic properties help to increase urine production, which can help to flush out bacteria that may be causing the infection. remedy_recipe: Harvest the solidago virgaurea leaves and flowers in late summer or early fall when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested solidago virgaurea leaves and flowers in a warm place for at least 24 hours.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried solidago virgaurea leaves and flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes allowing the solidago virgaurea to release its medicinal properties into the water.
Strain the tea and discard the solidago virgaurea leaves and flowers before serving the tea immediately.

remedy_properties: Uva Ursi (Bearberry): The leaf of this herb contains arbutin, a compound that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth in the urinary tract.

Marshmallow: Marshmallow root has been used for centuries to soothe bladder irritations and reduce inflammation. Its mucilages (thick, gel-like substances) can help protect the bladder lining from further irritation and reduce pain during urination.
Stinging Nettle: Stinging nettle leaf has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of cystitis, such as painful urination.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cucurbita pepo, also known as zucchini, teas helps with cystitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties and antioxidant content. The tea is derived from the seeds of the zucchini plant, which contain cucurbitacins, a group of compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause urinary tract infections. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, Cucurbita pepo tea may help alleviate symptoms of cystitis, such as pain and discomfort, and promote healing in the urinary tract.

Seeds: Cucurbita pepo seeds are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to help reduce inflammation and protect the urinary tract from damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 grams of dried cucurbita pepo flowers or young leaves in the early morning.
Steep the harvested cucurbita pepo flowers or young leaves in 250ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove solids.
Add honey to taste to the cooled tea if desired for a sweeter flavor.
Store any leftover tea in a sealed container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.

remedy_recipe: Gather 10-20 fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers for a full serving dosage.

Steep 1 teaspoon of taraxacum officinale in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 teaspoons of dried berberis vulgaris root and stem cuttings from a trusted supplier.
Grind the dried cuttings into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground berberis vulgaris with 1 cup of boiling water to create a strong decoction.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving it hot or chilled as desired.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Buchu tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as quercetin and kaempferol, help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of cystitis like burning and discomfort.

remedy_constituents: Citral: A terpene that has antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacterial infections that often cause cystitis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh or dried roots of this plant for a week's supply of tea.
Chop the roots into small pieces and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine the dried roots with 500ml of boiling water in a large teapot to make a strong tea infusion.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and medicinal properties to release into the water.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool before consuming 1-2 cups a day as needed.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and alleviate the discomfort associated with cystitis.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents in ginger tea, including citral and geraniol, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause urinary tract infections (UTIs), a common underlying cause of cystitis.
Spasmolytic properties: Ginger's spasmolytic compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help relax the muscles in the bladder and urethra, reducing spasms and pain associated with cystitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, teas helps with cystitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate cystitis symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 to 3 inches of fresh zingiber officinale roots in early morning when the dew is still present.
Wash the harvested roots with clean water to remove dirt and debris gently under running water.
Slice the washed roots into thin pieces weighing about 2 to 3 grams per 8 ounce cup of tea.
Steep the sliced roots in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea and serve hot or allow it to cool before drinking immediately after preparation.

Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial compounds have been found to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause cystitis, helping to prevent infections from occurring or recurring.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh vaccinium macrocarpon berries in late summer or early fall when ripe.
Rinse the berries with cold filtered water to remove any dirt or debris gently.
Combine 1/2 cup of the rinsed vaccinium macrocarpon berries with 2 cups of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the desired flavor and aroma are achieved slowly.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a large cup and discard the solids immediately.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried achillea millefolium flowers measuring 2 teaspoons for every 8 ounces of water.
Boil fresh or filtered water in a pot and let it cool down slightly to around 200°f for 3 minutes.
Steep the dried flowers in the cooled water for 5 to 7 minutes to release their bioactive compounds fully.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup discarding the solids completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with cystitis because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe the bladder and urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The flavonoids present in lavender tea have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause UTIs, helping to clear up infections faster. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender tea may help alleviate anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate cystitis symptoms, promoting a sense of relaxation and relief for those affected.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the urinary tract, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with cystitis.
Antibacterial property: The antibacterial properties of lavender tea help combat bacterial infections that often contribute to cystitis, reducing the risk of complications and promoting healing.

Sedative property: Lavender tea's calming effects can help soothe bladder spasms and reduce urgency to urinate, providing relief from the constant need to use the bathroom that is common with cystitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and ease urinary tract discomfort.
Seeds: Contain fatty acids that soothe and calm the bladder lining, reducing symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 quart of boiling water for the infusion process.
Steep the dried lavender flowers in hot water for 5-7 minutes to allow for optimal flavonoid extraction.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and preserve the delicate flavors.
Combine the infused liquid with honey to taste, if desired, and store it in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.
Drink the herbal tea in amounts of 1/2 cup twice daily, ideally before bedtime, to promote relaxation and reduce symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle teas helps with cystitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the burning sensation and discomfort associated with bladder inflammation. The tea's diuretic effects also help to increase urine production, allowing for more efficient removal of bacteria and toxins from the urinary tract. Additionally, stinging nettle's antiseptic and antimicrobial properties may help to reduce the severity of symptoms by combating bacterial infections that can cause cystitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of cystitis, including pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Nettle tea's antioxidant capabilities help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and tissue damage in the urinary tract, contributing to cystitis.

Diuretic properties: Stinging nettle tea's diuretic effects help increase urine production and promote the elimination of toxins from the body, which can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis, such as frequent urination and burning sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, making them effective in treating cystitis symptoms.
Buds: Nettle buds contain compounds that have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, helping to prevent bacterial infections and reduce oxidative stress associated with cystitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves or purchase dried nettle herb for the recipe.
Measure two teaspoons of dried nettle or one tablespoon of fresh nettle leaves for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the measured nettle and water in a medium saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes allowing the nettle's active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers teas helps with cystitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the bladder lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The tea's natural diuretic effects also help to flush out bacteria and toxins from the urinary tract, preventing further irritation and infection. Additionally, cleavers' antimicrobial compounds combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to cystitis symptoms, providing relief and promoting healing of the affected areas. Overall, cleavers teas offers a gentle and effective approach to managing cystitis symptoms naturally.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Cleavers tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and irritation in the urinary tract, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with cystitis.
Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial activity in cleavers tea helps combat bacterial infections that can cause cystitis, reducing the risk of complications and promoting healing.

Diuretic properties: Cleavers tea has a gentle diuretic effect, increasing urine production and helping to flush out bacteria, toxins, and other waste products from the urinary tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe urinary tract discomfort and reduce inflammation in the bladder.
Barks: The barks contain tannins, which provide astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and ease symptoms of cystitis, such as burning during urination.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh galium aparine leaves and flowers or purchase dried herbs from a reputable source.
Measure one teaspoon of dried galium aparine or two teaspoons of fresh galium aparine per cup of boiling water.
Steep the galium aparine in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its medicinal properties.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids, allowing it to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Drink the tea three times daily as needed, storing any remaining tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle teas helps with cystitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, work to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, providing relief from the pain and discomfort associated with cystitis. Additionally, roselle tea has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacterial infections that can contribute to cystitis. By soothing and calming the urinary tract, roselle tea offers a natural and effective way to alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Roselle tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins that help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with cystitis.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in Roselle tea, such as tannins and phenolic acids, can inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause urinary tract infections (UTIs), including E. coli, which is a common cause of cystitis.

Diuretic properties: Roselle tea has been traditionally used to increase urine production, which can help flush out bacteria and toxins from the urinary tract, reducing the risk of UTI recurrences and alleviating symptoms of cystitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with cystitis.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain bioactive compounds that inhibit bacterial growth, reducing the risk of infection and alleviating symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried hibiscus sabdariffa flowers for every cup of boiling water you plan to use.
Measure out the desired amount of dried hibiscus sabdariffa flowers and place them in a teapot or infuser.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the hibiscus sabdariffa flowers and allow it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature and consume immediately.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cystitis that are treated with herbal teas are urinary tract infections, bladder stones, and urethritis. Uva ursi tea has been traditionally used to treat UTIs due to its antimicrobial properties. Dandelion root tea helps reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of bladder stones, while nettle leaf tea soothes the bladder and urinary tract, providing relief from urethritis. These herbal teas may also help prevent future occurrences of these conditions.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for cystitis are natural remedies that help alleviate the symptoms of this common urinary tract infection. They work by reducing inflammation, fighting off bacteria, and promoting urination to flush out the infection. Examples of effective herbal teas include Uva Ursi, Juniper Berry, and Corn Silk, which have been used for centuries to soothe and calm cystitis symptoms. Drinking these teas can improve lives by reducing discomfort, frequency, and urgency, allowing people to manage their condition more effectively.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with cystitis is a blend of Marshmallow root, Uva Ursi, and Dandelion root. Marshmallow root soothes and protects the urinary tract, while Uva Ursi helps combat bacterial infections. Dandelion root aids in flushing out toxins and reducing inflammation. Drinking a combination of these teas can provide relief from cystitis symptoms, such as burning and pain during urination. Regular consumption can also promote overall urinary health and prevent future occurrences of cystitis. This blend can be enjoyed hot or iced.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, herbal teas have been found to be beneficial for cystitis due to their ability to reduce the recurrence rate of urinary tract infections (UTIs). The study, which analyzed 22 randomized controlled trials, found that traditional herbal medicines (THMs) led to significantly fewer UTI events in the experimental group than in the control group, with an odds ratio (OR) of 0.348 (95% CI = 0.257 to 0.473; p < 0.001). Furthermore, the study showed that THMs combined with antibiotics were effective in both the acute phase and follow-up period, indicating that herbal teas may be a helpful adjunctive treatment for cystitis.
